data.description:
## Why
`_mkdir_lock_is_stale` (`src/rebar/_store/lock.py:162-181`) has branches for read errors
(`except OSError: return False`), missing separator, foreign host, non-int PID
(`except ValueError: return False`), and live/dead PID. Existing tests cover dead/live/
absent/foreign-host, but **non-int PID, missing-`:`-separator, and owner-file read-error are
untested** (PARTIALLY-VALIDATED: a real partial-branch coverage gap). The safety property is
conservative — none may cause reclamation.

## What
Add table-driven cases for the malformed/unreadable owner-file forms.

## Scope
- `tests/unit/test_lock_stale_reclaim.py`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Cases: empty owner file; missing colon; empty host or PID; non-numeric PID; negative
      PID; extra delimiters; unreadable owner file; owner file replaced while read.
- [ ] Every case is conservative: no reclamation occurs.
- [ ] `make test` green.
data.title: Add table-driven mkdir-lock malformed ownership-stamp tests
